creator: itetcu@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Sat, 7 May 2011 16:40:13 GMT
Juergen Lock <nox@jelal.kn-bremen.de> wrote:

> The following reply was made to PR ports/156860; it has been noted by
> GNATS.
> 
> From: Juergen Lock <nox@jelal.kn-bremen.de>
> To: Ion-Mihai Tetcu <itetcu@FreeBSD.org>
> Cc: Juergen Lock <nox@jelal.kn-bremen.de>, Andriy Gapon
> <avg@icyb.net.ua>, bug-followup@FreeBSD.org, nox@FreeBSD.org
> Subject: Re: ports/156860: [PATCH] net/skype: update to 2.2.0.25
> Date: Sat, 7 May 2011 18:26:49 +0200
> 
>  On Sat, May 07, 2011 at 05:24:48PM +0300, Ion-Mihai Tetcu wrote:
>  > What I would like to hear before touching net/skype is that any of
>  > these updates have sound working, reliable and not only on HEAD.
>  > The very outdated and google-for-distfile version that it is
>  > currently at least allows for voice communication, which is the
>  > main reason for using skype.
>  > 
>  > I have a few patches locally that update to various versions,
>  > including some internal builds, but I never got sound working
>  > reliably on 8-STABLE (last time tried some 2 months ago).
>  > 
>  Yes currently you still need to manually merge the one-line fix
>  from r221388, and even after that's been committed to stable, people
>  on releases will still have to manually apply it and (at least)
>  rebuild and reload their sound.ko.

This should be MCed today/tomorrow, I guess only in 8? We need fc10 for
skype so it won't which AFAIR won't run on 7 anyway. I think we can
live with restricting newer skype versions to 8-STABLE after some point
and HEAD.

>  > Video/screen sharing and Skype Access support are a very nice to
>  > have bonus, but as long as we don't have good sound ...
>  > 
>   video does work with the old version too btw, only not with the
>  very latest one.
>  
>  > IMO the port should be moved to net-im, no?
>  > 
>  > And I agree it's a good idea to keep at least 2 versions, possibly
>  > 3.

OK, then I'll ask for the following repocopies:
- net/skype12 --> net-im/skype12 (could we maybe nuke this?)
- net/skype --> net-im/skype20 (will remain at the current version)
- net/skype --> net-im/skype (2.1.0.81)
- net/skype-devel --> net-im/skype-devel (2.2.0.25)

BTW, be prepared for a few new linux dependencies once the next version
comes out.

While here, does anyone happen to know how do we stay WRT BlueZ
compatibility?

-- 
IOnut - Un^d^dregistered ;) FreeBSD "user"
  "Intellectual Property" is   nowhere near as valuable   as "Intellect"
FreeBSD committer -> itetcu@FreeBSD.org, PGP Key ID 057E9F8B493A297B
